characters.name: Henry White
characters.appearance: A man who feels the cold and experiences physical reactions to fear, such as trembling hands and a hammering heart.
characters.wants: Security for his wife and son; initially money to pay debts, later the return of his son, and finally peace from the supernatural threat.
characters.emotional_state: Despairing, terrified, and ultimately resigned to grief and fate.

characters.name: Mrs. White
characters.appearance: A woman whose face turns white as moonlight or ash; her eyes burn with frantic fire or become hollow pits.
characters.wants: To bring her son Herbert back to life.
characters.emotional_state: Initially placid, then desperate and mad with grief, finally defeated and hollow.

characters.name: Herbert White
characters.appearance: Described as having sparkling eyes with mischief; later revealed to be dead with mangled fingers.
characters.wants: To laugh at his father's hesitation and play the piano; implicitly wants security for his family.
characters.emotional_state: Mischief and laughter (early); dead and silent (later).

characters.name: Morris
characters.appearance: Tall, burly, with a red face from cold travel; eyes wide with fear later on.
characters.wants: To warn the Whites about the Monkey's Paw and have them leave it alone.
characters.emotional_state: Grave, serious, fearful of the consequences.

locations.name: Laburnam Villa
locations.description: A house with heavy debts, a fireplace grate, a mantel, and a front door. It is described as quiet, dark, and eventually secure but haunted by grief.

locations.name: Hallway
locations.description: An endless and dark space leading to the front door where the knocking occurs.

chekhov_objects.name: The Monkey's Paw
chekhov_objects.description: A dried mummy paw, shriveled and twisted like a snake, said to be enchanted by a fakir. It grants three wishes but brings sorrowful consequences.

chekhov_objects.name: Letter from Maw and Meggins
chekhov_objects.description: A paper containing the news of Herbert's death by machinery and an offer of two hundred pounds in compensation.

secrets.content: Herbert White died when his fingers were caught in machinery, mangled beyond recognition.
